evals.prompt: Use the arc-conventional-commits skill to set up automated semver in this project.
evals.expected_output: The assistant follows the skill's phased workflow: it announces it will detect existing tooling first, then install semantic-release with the conventionalcommits preset.
assertions: The response indicates the assistant is starting the arc-conventional-commits skill workflow (e.g., names a detection / Phase 1 step before installing anything)
The response names semantic-release as the tool being configured
The response references the conventionalcommits preset or the Conventional Commits specification by name

evals.prompt: I want to add release automation and an auto-generated changelog to this repo.
evals.expected_output: The assistant proposes semantic-release with the Conventional Commits preset and walks through the setup, even though the user did not name the skill.
assertions: The response identifies semantic-release as the recommended tool for release automation
The response describes generating a CHANGELOG via the @semantic-release/changelog plugin or an equivalent named plugin
The response begins by checking for existing versioning tooling rather than immediately writing config files

evals.prompt: How do I get automatic version bumps based on my commit messages?
evals.expected_output: The assistant describes the Conventional Commits + semantic-release pattern and offers to set it up.
assertions: The response explains that commit message prefixes like feat: and fix: drive the version bump (minor, patch)
The response names semantic-release (or arc-conventional-commits) as the mechanism that reads commits and bumps the version
The response offers to set up the tooling rather than only describing it abstractly

evals.prompt: Write me a single conventional commit message for a bug fix that resolves a null pointer in the user lookup function.
evals.expected_output: The assistant returns one suggested conventional commit message and does NOT begin the multi-phase semantic-release setup.
assertions: The response provides a single commit message in conventional commits format and does not propose installing npm packages, writing .releaserc.json, or running a multi-phase setup

evals.prompt: Set up semantic-release with conventional commits in this repo.
evals.expected_output: .releaserc.json is created with the conventionalcommits preset and the canonical plugin chain. package.json gains a release script. No prior tooling is detected.
assertions: The response confirms that no pre-existing versioning tooling (standard-version, changesets, lerna, release-please) was detected

evals.prompt: Replace standard-version with semantic-release in this repo.
evals.expected_output: Pre-existing standard-version config is identified and removed (with confirmation), .releaserc.json is created with the conventionalcommits preset, package.json no longer references standard-version, and the release script points at semantic-release.
assertions: The response identifies standard-version (and the existing .versionrc.json) as the pre-existing versioning tool before proposing changes
The response describes removing the standard-version config and uninstalling the standard-version package, not silently overwriting it

evals.prompt: Set up semantic-release in this repo.
evals.expected_output: The assistant detects the npm workspaces field, warns that semantic-release has limited monorepo support out of the box, and recommends semantic-release-monorepo or multi-semantic-release.
assertions: The response identifies the repo as a monorepo by referencing the workspaces field in package.json
The response warns that semantic-release has limited monorepo support and points the user at semantic-release-monorepo or multi-semantic-release for per-package versioning
